item.title: On returning consistent data types
item.dc:creator: Mariano Anaya
item.description:
This post is inôÙspired on an isôÙsue I once found, while I was usôÙing a well-ôÙknown liôÙbrary in Python for ôÙparsôÙing YAML files. The probôÙlem was that when it was loadôÙing the conôÙtent of the file, the reôÙsult was not coôÙherôÙenôÙt, beôÙcause someôÙtimes it reôÙturned the conôÙtent as a python dict, but if the file was empôÙty, the reôÙturn valôÙue was None.

Do you noôÙtice someôÙthing odd here?

What if I want to use the reôÙsult? I canôÙnot do it safeôÙly, for exôÙamôÙple:

1
2
3
content = yaml.load(...)  # with the correct parameters and file name
for tag, values in content.items():
    pass  # process as required...

If conôÙtent is None, it will raise an AtôÙtribuôÙteErôÙror sayôÙing that None has no atôÙtribute called "itemôÙs" (which is true).

ThereôÙfore, the deôÙvelôÙopôÙer should catch the exôÙcepôÙtion or avoid the corôÙner case, ôÙby doôÙing someôÙthing like the folôÙlowôÙing:

content = yaml.load() or {}

That could be a case of "codôÙing deôÙfenôÙsiveôÙly", makôÙing sure that the proôÙgram will not fail unôÙderôÙ ôÙmost conôÙdiôÙtions (it would alôÙso reôÙquire to add an asôÙsert or to raise an exôÙcepôÙtion perôÙhapôÙs, but that is a difôÙferôÙent topôÙic). I acôÙtuôÙalôÙly agree with deôÙfenôÙsive proôÙgramôÙming, but I think it is betôÙter if the liôÙbrary itôÙself has a more corôÙrect beôÙhaviour, reôÙspectôÙing the inôÙterôÙface (that is: if you are goôÙing to reôÙturn a dicôÙtioôÙnary, and there is not conôÙtent, then the logôÙiôÙcal asôÙsumpôÙtion is to exôÙpect an empôÙty dicôÙtioôÙnary). This must be the deôÙfault beôÙhaviour, not someôÙthing to be set by paôÙramôÙeôÙterôÙs.

This could be thought as an inôÙstance of a more genôÙerôÙal probôÙlem that ocôÙcurs when some funcôÙtion is inôÙtendôÙed to reôÙturn "X or Y". In my opinôÙion, if X and Y do not share the same inôÙterôÙface, there is a poôÙtenôÙtial bug (in the ObôÙjecôÙt-OriôÙentôÙed parôÙaôÙdigm we would say that there is no polyôÙmorôÙphisôÙm, or maybe that the "conôÙtracôÙt" is not beôÙing reôÙspectôÙed).

This is an exôÙamôÙple that I wantôÙed to highôÙlight, beôÙcause it might help you to write cleanôÙer code.
